ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly identifies Piccolo. The vision described a tall green-skinned humanoid with white cape, weighted turban, and pointed ears with arms crossed — these are Piccolo's definitive signature visual identifiers canonical across all Dragon Ball media. Confirmed via Dragon Ball Wiki and Wikipedia.

trivia_bullets.text: At the 25th World Martial Arts Tournament, Piccolo qualified for the adult bracket but forfeited his match against the Supreme Kai (Shin) without fighting — intimidated by Shin's divine power, which Piccolo could sense was far beyond his own. He then accompanied Shin to pursue Babidi's forces. [Dragon Ball Wiki: World Tournament Saga; Wikipedia: Piccolo (Dragon Ball)]

Piccolo is one of TB02's Uncommon pulls (TB2-027) — a step above the Common-tier henchmen cards in the same set. His arc in the Buu Saga is one of mentorship: after the tournament, he trained Goten and Trunks for the Fusion technique, and was eventually absorbed by Majin Buu alongside Gotenks, temporarily granting Buu tactical intelligence. [Wikipedia: Piccolo (Dragon Ball)]

The 'Begrudging Respect' title maps precisely to Piccolo's relationship with Gohan: Piccolo began as Goku's arch-enemy and Gohan's reluctant kidnapper, and became his most devoted mentor. By the Buu Saga, that relationship had evolved into something the series never quite put a clean label on — the card title does that work in two words. Community sentiment on r/dragonball considers Piccolo/Gohan one of the most emotionally resonant dynamics in shonen. [Wikipedia: Piccolo (Dragon Ball)]
